title: Maple Heights
description: First suburban village. A shopkeeper who stocks medieval items and knows about the portal. A mail carrier who's seen the appliances go rogue. A lawn-watering man who thinks Thursday is the answer to everything. A kid on a bicycle with opinions about cosplay.

steps.text: Maple Heights existed in a state of aggressive normalcy. Every house was a variation on the same house. Every lawn was the same shade of green. The sky was blue, but it was the wrong blue — not wrong in a way you could point to, but wrong in a way you could feel.

lines.speaker: Kid on Bicycle
lines.text: The mall has a Spirit Halloween now. Seasonal, obviously. You could probably get better armor there. No offense.

lines.speaker: Surplus Shopkeeper
lines.text: Everything's half off. We got a huge shipment in from... I don't know, the warehouse? It just shows up.

lines.speaker: Surplus Shopkeeper
lines.text: From wherever. I just ring them up. You want the potions? They're the only things that actually work. The weapons are decorative, which I am legally required to say.

lines.speaker: Surplus Shopkeeper
lines.text: You're from the other side of the portal, aren't you. Don't worry about it. People come through sometimes. You get used to it.

lines.speaker: Surplus Shopkeeper
lines.text: The HOA keeps tabs on everything. The Coordinator is going to find you within the day. Stock up first.

lines.speaker: Mail Carrier
lines.text: It's been like this for months. Appliances going rogue. Kids disappearing — not missing, just relocated. The HOA says it's 'community restructuring.' Nobody questions it because nobody questions the HOA.
